title: [Bug]: Encryption envelope parser accepts duplicate JSON keys
summary: The payload encryption envelope parser uses default JSON parsing and can silently accept duplicate keys before decrypting a payload.
observed: A JSON envelope containing two encryption-envelope keys can be parsed with the final one silently winning.
expected: Encrypted payload envelopes should reject duplicate keys as ambiguous before any decryption or integrity checks run.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that constructs an encrypted payload envelope with duplicate root keys and calls decrypt_payload.
impact: Encryption envelopes are security-sensitive boundary data. Ambiguous parsing could let a stored payload differ from what operators or downstream validation expect.
delivery_semantics: The fix must not change ACK ordering. Invalid encrypted payload inspection should raise a framework serialization error when a caller explicitly decrypts it.
security: The fix must not log plaintext, ciphertext, key material, nonces, tags, or full envelope contents.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves duplicate encryption envelope JSON keys are rejected.
Valid encrypted payloads still decrypt successfully.
Error messages do not include crypto material.
tests: Run the focused regression test, encryption tests, payload tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and encryption documentation to mention strict envelope parsing.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
